content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


July 31, 1986


Mr. D. R. Powers
Post Office Box 365
Davis, CA 95617-0365

Re: FPPC Advice No. A-86-221

Dear Mr. Powers:

Thank you for your letter of July 9, 1986, requesting advice
concerning reporting requirements contained in the campaign
disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (Government
Code Sections 81000-91015).

FACTS

You are the treasurer for a recipient committee which is
primarily formed to defeat a measure that qualified for the
November 4, 1986, Davis city ballot. The measure will only be
voted on by the citizens registered to vote within the Davis
city limits.

You recently received a letter from the Yolo County Clerk's
office instructing you to file the committee's campaign
statements with the county.

QUESTIONS

You have asked for clarification as to where you should file
the committee's campaign disclosure statements. In addition,
you asked if the name of your committee, "A Committee Against
Davis Measure ___" is acceptable under the requirements of the
Political Reform Act.

ANSWERS

I contacted the Yolo County Clerk's office and spoke to Ms.
Donna Blankenship. I explained to her that the committees which
are primarily formed to support or oppose the Davis measure file
campaign statements with the City Clerk's office in Davis and
not with the county.

In regard to your second question, beginning January 1, 1986,
committees primarily formed to support or oppose a ballot
measure must include a statement "a committee for measure ____"

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Mr. D. R. Powers
Page Two


or "a committee against Measure ____" as a part of its committee
name. The example you provided in your letter fulfills this
requirement so long as the measure's letter or number is also
provided. Enclosed for your information is FPPC Fact Sheet C-7
which gives various examples of this new reporting requirement.

Please do not hesitate to contact me if I may be of
assistance in the future.

Sincerely,

Mary Ann Kvasager
Political Reform Consultant

MAK:ch
Enclosure

FPFC
JUL 10 8 44AM '86

Post Office Box 365
Davis, CA 95617-0365
9 July 1986


Jeanne Pritchard, Chief
Technical Assistance and Analysis Branch
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Dear Ms. Pritchard:

I am treasurer for a receipient committee primarily organized to defeat a
measure that qualified for the November 4, 1986, ballot via initiative petition
circulated within the City of Davis. This measure will only be voted on by
citizens registered to vote within the Davis city limits.

My understanding is that my committee is required to file campaign disclosure
statements with the city filing officer on the timetable set forth in the
Political Reform Act of 1974, as amended. I also understand that we do not
have a filing duty to the County of Yolo, because the measure we oppose is
strictly a Davis municipal matter being voted on at a special municipal
election consolidated with the November General Election.

However, I recently received a letter from the Yolo County Clerk's office
indicating the county filing officer expects a campaign disclosure statement
from my committee by July 31, 1986, for the period january 1 to June 30, 1986.

I spoke with Alice Hughes in your office today to clarify this matter. She
indicated someone in your office would contact the county to inform them they
are not due a copy of our campaign disclosure statement. Ms. Hughes also
said I may ask for a statement from your office to document the fact I checked
and do not have to file a statement with the county. I would appreciate it
if you would send such a letter indicating our compliance.

I would also like to request a written opinion on the validity of my committee
using the phrase "A Committee Against Davis Measure __" as part of our name,
as opposed to the phrase "A Committee Against Measure __."

Thank you very much for the time and attention of you and your staff.

Sincerely,

D R Powers

P.S. Please call me at (916) 756-9433 if you have any questions.
